# Free Internet VPN: Best Free VPN with Unlimited Data in 2026

By

Florian Gray
Published February 19, 2026, 2:00 am ET


![Best Free Vpn With Unlimited Data](https://gizmodo.com/app/uploads/2025/04/Best-free-VPN-with-unlimited-data.jpg)© Gizmodo.com

A typical free internet VPN usually comes with strings attached. A much dreaded data cap, privacy issues, slow speeds, and more. But imagine this: You get halfway through your favorite series, and bam: “ **_You’ve reached your monthly limit._**” Sorry. You’ll have to get back to it next month. Hope you’re patient.

To avoid this issue, people seek the best unlimited free internet VPN, which actually doesn’t have a data cap. A VPN free internet isn’t a scam. Some providers offer it, and while they do skimp on other features, at least you can browse and stream endlessly. We tested over 20 free internet VPN providers in 2026.

At the end, we were shocked at how bad most were. However, our list isn’t empty, as we still managed to pull out five free internet VPN options that truly work and are safe. Excited? So are we. Here’s our shortlist. More information is available below.

Table of Contents

## Best Free Internet VPN: Security, Unlimited Data, and Fast Speeds

1. [**NordVPN**](https://shop.gizmodo.com/go/2114/) – The best free internet VPN trial (free for 30 days)
2. [**Proton VPN**](https://shop.gizmodo.com/go/10/) – Safe and secure unlimited free internet VPN
3. **Hide.me** – The safest VPN that gives free data (no limit)
4. **PrivadoVPN** – The best free internet VPN for streaming
5. **Hotspot Shield** – Unlimited free internet VPN for Android and iOS

## Best Unlimited Free Internet VPN: Detailed List

Here’s the thing about free VPNs: most of them give you just enough data to almost do what you want. Watch a few YouTube videos, check your email, and stream a single episode if you are lucky. After that? You’re cut off unless you pony up for a paid plan.

But we found the exceptions—VPNs that give you real, usable protection and **unlimited data** without locking everything behind a paywall. Some are 100% free forever. Others, like our first pick, give you a sneaky-smart way to access full premium features without paying a cent (at least for 30 days). Let’s start at the top.

### 1\. NordVPN: Best Free Internet VPN Overall (Free for 30 Days)

![Nordvpn Free Trial](https://gizmodo.com/app/uploads/2026/01/NordVPN-Free-Trial.jpg)© NordVPN

Pros

- Double VPN servers
- Works with Netflix
- Torrenting is allowed
- No bandwidth cap
- Unlimited internet data
- Audited no-log policy

Cons

- Not free forever
- Confusing app UI

NordVPN is hands-down the best way to get unlimited VPN access without paying upfront. While it’s not forever free, it offers two generous, no-strings-attached options to use the full service free: A **3-day free trial on Android** or a **30-day money-back guarantee**, which you get after subscribing. Both are pretty good.

As a whole, NordVPN is better than any free internet VPN. Unlimited data is essential, but you get much faster speeds as NordVPN runs on its NordLynx protocol. Speed losses aren’t at all noticeable, and NordVPN even **works with streaming services** (Netflix included), and offers **P2P servers for endless torrenting**.

Security and privacy are some of the most advanced. NordLynx, its in-house protocol, uses post-quantum encryption, and NordVPN backs it up with Double VPN routing, **malware and tracker protection**, and a kill switch. It doesn’t store any logs either, so it’s a standout free internet [VPN for top-to-bottom privacy](https://gizmodo.com/best-vpn/privacy).

You’ll notice that NordVPN is versatile and works for all purposes, with advanced features, no data cap, and apps for all devices. It even **supports ten simultaneous connections**. But it’s not free forever, and the cheapest [biennial Basic plan is $3.39 per month](https://shop.gizmodo.com/go/2114/). That’s a very low price that makes it almost free.

[Try NordVPN risk-free for 30 days](https://shop.gizmodo.com/go/2114/)

* * *

### 2\. Proton VPN: Best Truly Free Internet VPN With Unlimited Data

![Proton Vpn Free](https://gizmodo.com/app/uploads/2025/09/Proton-VPN-Free.jpg)© Proton VPN

Pros

- Unlimited free data
- No credit card required
- Powerful security
- Audited no-logs policy

Cons

- Can’t choose the server
- Torrenting is blocked
- No simultaneous connections

[If you’re looking for a VPN that’s actually free](https://gizmodo.com/best-vpn/free) and offers unlimited data with no time limit, Proton VPN checks almost all the boxes. It’s an unlimited free internet VPN with no data or bandwidth caps, so it’s still fast. Being a Swiss provider, it has a no-log policy and actually **doesn’t collect or sell your data**.

Its apps work on all systems, and even the free version includes WireGuard, leak protection, and a kill switch. Users get access to ten server locations, and since they include **the USA, Japan, and a few other European countries**, the network is robust. Sadly, some corners are cut, and Proton VPN isn’t that amazing.

For instance, you can’t use your free internet VPN data for torrenting; **it’s blocked**. Also, you will notice that **streaming services mostly don’t work**, although it unblocks some less famous TV channels. The free plan doesn’t let you choose the server location, so you’re just randomly connected to the fastest one.

[As stated in our Proton VPN review](https://gizmodo.com/best-vpn/proton-vpn), the premium version doesn’t do this. To distance its free version from the premium even more, the company also excluded simultaneous connections and its 24/7 support. [Proton VPN Premium is similar to NordVPN quality-wise](https://gizmodo.com/best-vpn/proton-vpn-vs-nordvpn), but its VPN free internet offer is **_light-years away from it_**.

[Try Proton VPN](https://shop.gizmodo.com/go/10/)

* * *

### 3\. Hide.me: Best Free Internet VPN for Security

![Hide.me Free Vpn](https://gizmodo.com/app/uploads/2026/01/Hide.me-Free-VPN.jpg)© Hide.me

Pros

- Free data forever
- WireGuard protocol
- Good for light streaming
- Easy-to-use app

Cons

- Blocked torrenting
- 8 server locations
- Occasional slow speed

Hide.me used to be known for its tight 10 GB cap, but not anymore. **Free and premium users now both get unlimited data**, so the provider doesn’t hold you back that much. The free plan includes eight server locations; decent coverage for browsing, casual streaming, or watching YouTube videos unrestricted.

Hide.me is relatively fast, as it uses WireGuard and isn’t frequently overcrowded. Even better, the free version doesn’t restrict protocol access, so even its **SoftEther protocol** works if you need to bypass censorship. Hide.me doesn’t keep logs and uses the **latest technologies for your privacy and security online**.

Now, it does have a few limits. [We tested Hide.me](https://gizmodo.com/best-vpn/hide-me), and its premium version downloaded all torrents effortlessly. **The free version blocks torrenting** and won’t work with streamers like Netflix, BBC iPlayer, and Disney+. Additionally, **simultaneous connections won’t work**, and you won’t get any advanced features.

On the flip side, **it unblocks VoIP calls and is smooth enough for basic tasks**. We chose this unlimited free internet VPN mostly for its security and reliability, but if you want more, you have to splurge on the premium plan. If you’re going that route, you may as well get NordVPN, our #1 free internet VPN.

* * *

### 4\. PrivadoVPN: Best Free Internet VPN for Streaming

![Privadovpn Free](https://gizmodo.com/app/uploads/2025/09/PrivadoVPN-Free.jpg)© PrivadoVPN

Pros

- Free emergency servers
- Torrenting is allowed
- Streaming sites work

Cons

- 10 GB high-speed data cap
- Small server list
- No P2P on emergency servers
- No third-party audits

PrivadoVPN is a rare gem in the free VPN space. To be clear, it’s a free internet VPN, but it may not seem like it. It openly states that it allows 10 GB of data every month. However, it offers **unlimited access to free emergency servers** after you hit your limit. That means you are never fully cut off from the internet.

At the start, you get **10 GB of high-speed data**, and this data works for torrenting or even streaming on Netflix. PrivadoVPN is a famous [free VPN working on Netflix](https://gizmodo.com/best-vpn/free/netflix), by the way. When not using emergency servers, you can **pick from 12 server locations**, including the US, the UK, Canada, France, Mexico, and more.

Being a Swiss provider, PrivadoVPN doesn’t keep logs and is actually good for your privacy. It also uses WireGuard, so it’s fast, and relies on **256-bit encryption and a kill switch**. Its free version lacks a few unique features, but the free internet VPN is still secure. PrivadoVPN is beginner-friendly and works on all devices.

Sadly, it lacks simultaneous connections, **doesn’t have third-party audits**, and doesn’t allow torrenting or streaming on its emergency servers. In fact, they’re **pretty slow**, as well, serving only basic functions, i.e., browsing. [Read our PrivadoVPN review](https://gizmodo.com/best-vpn/privadovpn) to learn more and see why it’s a worthy unlimited free internet VPN.

* * *

### 5\. Hotspot Shield: Best Free Internet VPN for Mobile Phones

![Hotspot Shield Free Vpn](https://gizmodo.com/app/uploads/2025/04/Hotspot-Shield-Free-VPN.jpg)© Hotspot Shield

Pros

- Free data on iOS & Android
- Fast US VPN server

Cons

- Possible logging
- In-app targeted ads
- Only US servers
- No streaming

Hotspot Shield is an unlimited free internet VPN for Android and iOS. Its free data VPN offer doesn’t apply to Windows and macOS. However, on mobile phones, you get no data cap, but you must **watch ads every 15 or so minutes**. That doesn’t bother you? Good, because this is a fast and reliable VPN with limitless data.

It also includes a kill switch, 256-bit encryption, and **Hydra**, its protocol for bypassing strong censorship and unblocking content. Hotspot Shield’s apps are stunningly good and are very easy to set up. However, you’re limited to a US server, which can be overcrowded, resulting in **slower, less stable connections**.

When testing Hotspot Shield, its unlimited free internet allowed us to access restricted TV channels, but not streaming platforms. **Torrenting worked**, but was very slow. [Our Hotspot Shield review](https://gizmodo.com/best-vpn/hotspot-shield) expressed privacy concerns, however. Its privacy policy states that it **may store logs of some of your sensitive information**.

This is typical for a VPN that gives free data, so don’t be surprised. At the end of the day, it’s the best VPN for free internet if you want **sufficient speed, solid security, and reliable US-only servers**. However, don’t think [Hotspot Shield can rival NordVPN](https://gizmodo.com/best-vpn/hotspot-shield-vs-nordvpn). It’s miles behind, which is why it took the last spot on the list.

* * *

## Factors to Consider When Choosing a Free Internet VPN

Not all free VPNs are worth the download. While the services we covered above are reliable, secure, and (most importantly) transparent, many free VPNs e come with serious trade-offs, including shady logging policies, intrusive ads, or even flat-out malware. Here’s what to keep in mind before committing to a free VPN:

### 📈 Monthly Data Limits

Plenty of so-called free Internet VPN providers actually limit your monthly data. Just because you get a free data VPN, it doesn’t mean that data is unlimited. Some providers offer 2 GB, like TunnelBear, while others, like Windscribe, cap at 10 GB. An unlimited free internet VPN should not impose any limits.

This is essential for normal internet use besides checking your mail or using a data-saving version of the social media app once in a while (like Facebook Lite).

### 🌎 Server Network Access

Free VPNs often restrict you to just one or two servers. That can lead to overcrowding, slow speeds, and zero flexibility for accessing geo-restricted content. Services like Proton VPN and Hide.me offer a bit more variety, even on the free plan.

### 📝 Privacy Policy (No-Log Policy)

You’re using a VPN to stay private—so if the provider logs your data, you’re defeating the purpose. Always choose services with a **strict no-logs policy**, ideally one that’s been independently audited. NordVPN, Proton VPN, and Hide.me all check this box, while Hotspot Shield is on the flimsier side.

### ⚡ Speed & Stability

[VPNs can slow down your internet](https://gizmodo.com/best-vpn/does-vpn-slow-down-internet), especially if the service is overloaded or throttled for free users. NordVPN’s free trial gives you full-speed access, while Hide.me and Proton VPN offer solid speeds without sneaky slowdowns.

### ⚙️ Features & Platform Support

Look for must-have security tools like a **kill switch, split tunneling**, and support for **modern VPN protocols** like WireGuard or NordLynx in NordVPN. Device compatibility also matters. All the VPNs in our list work across Windows, macOS, Android, and iOS. Some unlimited free internet VPN providers also block torrenting.

So, you won’t be able to rely on them to [download torrents in a safe and secure way](https://gizmodo.com/best-vpn/how-to-torrent-safely). If this is an issue, again, opt for NordVPN, which puts zero restrictions on this activity.

### 💯 Trustworthiness & Reputation

Some free VPNs exist solely to harvest your data. That’s why we left out:

- [**Hola VPN**](https://gizmodo.com/best-vpn/hola-vpn), which uses a peer-to-peer network, can expose your IP and bandwidth to strangers. It’s the most dangerous free internet VPN you can use.
- **VPN Proxy Master** and **Turbo VPN** with questionable privacy policies and vague ownership details. Some of the worst providers for your privacy.
- **Betternet**, which has been caught embedding trackers and serving targeted ads.

If a VPN is free but offers no transparency about how it operates, what it logs, or who owns it, just skip it. You’re better off with a limited but honest service than a flashy app that’s actually selling your data in the background.

## Final Words

A free data VPN is only useful if it offers unlimited data. Strict caps, like those of TunnelBear or Windscribe, won’t get you far, especially when streaming, working remotely, or torrenting. But even with an unlimited free internet VPN, you may face some compromises in security, speed, server access, and compatibility.

That said, the best free data VPN with no compromises is NordVPN. It’s not free forever, but a [NordVPN free trial](https://gizmodo.com/best-vpn/nordvpn/free-trial) lets you test it for 3 or 30 days without losing money. When compared to 100% free options, **_NordVPN is simply a cut or two above_**. Can’t afford it? In that case, you still have a few great choices.

We emphasize **Proton VPN and Hide.me as truly free internet VPN options** without any data caps, for endless browsing and occasional streaming. PrivadoVPN and Hotspot Shield may be less ideal, but still good if your expectations are in check.

[Try NordVPN risk-free now](https://shop.gizmodo.com/go/2114/)

* * *

## Frequently Asked Questions

### What’s the best unlimited free internet VPN for Android?

The best unlimited free internet VPN for Android is **NordVPN** if you don’t mind using it for free temporarily. If you need something that lasts forever, consider **Proton VPN**, which offers no data restrictions and doesn’t display ads in the app.

### What’s the best VPN for free internet?

The best VPN for free internet is **Proton VPN**. It’s a Swiss-based service with top-tier privacy, robust security, and unlimited data VPN connections that truly work. Just keep in mind that you can’t choose the server, and it blocks torrenting.

### How to get free data with a VPN?

**Get a free internet VPN with unlimited data**. Opt for Proton VPN or Hide.me. Both have zero data limits and even offer unrestricted bandwidth, so your connection speed is never capped. But be aware that they limit torrenting and streaming.

All the VPNs in our list work across Windows, macOS, Android, and iOS. Some unlimited free internet VPN providers also block torrenting.

So, you won’t be able to rely on them to download torrents in a safe and secure way. If this is an issue, again, opt for NordVPN, which puts zero restrictions on this activity.

💯 Trustworthiness & Reputation

Some free VPNs exist solely to harvest your data. That’s why we left out:

  • Hola VPN, which uses a peer-to-peer network, can expose your IP and bandwidth to strangers. It’s the most dangerous free internet VPN you can use.
  • VPN Proxy Master and Turbo VPN with questionable privacy policies and vague ownership details. Some of the worst providers for your privacy.
  • Betternet, which has been caught embedding trackers and serving targeted ads.

If a VPN is free but offers no transparency about how it operates, what it logs, or who owns it, just skip it. You’re better off with a limited but honest service than a flashy app that’s actually selling your data in the background.

Final Words

A free data VPN is only useful if it offers unlimited data. Strict caps, like those of TunnelBear or Windscribe, won’t get you far, especially when streaming, working remotely, or torrenting. But even with an unlimited free internet VPN, you may face some compromises in security, speed, server access, and compatibility.

That said, the best free data VPN with no compromises is NordVPN. It’s not free forever, but a NordVPN free trial lets you test it for 3 or 30 days without losing money. When compared to 100% free options, NordVPN is simply a cut or two above. Can’t afford it? In that case, you still have a few great choices.

We emphasize Proton VPN and Hide.me as truly free internet VPN options without any data caps, for endless browsing and occasional streaming. PrivadoVPN and Hotspot Shield may be less ideal, but still good if your expectations are in check.
